{{Quote|Silence, foolish droid! Be quiet or I will have you sold for scrap!|Wimateeka losing his temper with [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]]|<ref name="DroidWorks">''[[Star Wars: DroidWorks]]''</ref>}}
'''Wimateeka''' was the [[Jawa/Legends|Jawa]] leader of the [[Nkik clan]].

==Biography==
Wimateeka was a friend of [[Moisture farmer/Legends|moisture farmer]] [[Ariq Joanson]]. He taught Ariq [[Jawaese/Legends|Jawaese]] and played an instrumental role in Ariq's plans to make peace between settlers, Jawas, and the [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raiders]]. At some point, Wimateeka and his clan was invited to [[Eyvind/Legends|Eyvind]] and [[Ariela]]'s [[Marriage/Legends|wedding]]. About 31 members arrived at the wedding, bringing with them rock salt, a tiny [[Droid/Legends|droid]], cloth, and [[Water/Legends|water]]. The wedding was later attacked by a group of young Tusken Raiders attempting to steal a [[Landspeeder/Legends|speeder]]. In the resulting confusion, Eyvind was [[Death/Legends|killed]] and Ariela was captured. Wimateeka assisted in the negotiations for Ariela's release by translating the sand people language for Ariq.<ref name="Drawing">{{StoryCite|story=Drawing the Maps of Peace: The Moisture Farmer's Tale|book=Tales from the Mos Eisley Cantina}}</ref>

In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]], Wimateeka's clan was attacked by [[Human/Legends|Humans]] and [[Alien/Legends|aliens]] and several Jawas were killed in the raid. In retaliation, Wimateeka hired a well-armed individual to destroy the base or their enemies.<ref name="SWG:JK">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=terminal||Jawa Killers}} on Tatooine</ref>

On another occasion, Wimateeka contracted a number of hunters to eliminate wild animals that were attacking his clan's supply of [[Hubba gourd/Legends|hubba gourds]].<ref name="SWG:HGR">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=terminal||Hubba Gourd Raiders}} on Tatooine</ref>

Wimateeka also had been known to work with the [[Holocam E]] and a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Unidentified Rebel agent (droid builder)|agent]] to stop the Empire from creating assassin droids on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]].<ref name="DroidWorks" />

==Personality and traits==
Wimateeka was as passionate as he was wise. Depending on the circumstance, his strong feelings shone through, be it exuberance over a job well done or snappishness over disruptions. C-3PO learned this latter the hard way, when Wimateeka threatened to scrap the protocol droid if he did not be quiet. He appeared to care for the galaxy at large and sympathized with the Alliance to Restore the Republic in their struggle against the Empire.He was able to speak both [[Jawaese/Legends|Jawaese]] and [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]].<ref name="DroidWorks" />

==Behind the scenes==
The instruction manual for the ''[[Star Wars: DroidWorks]]'' game refers to Wimateeka as the leader of the "Olik" clan, which contradicts other sources.<ref name="DroidWorks" />
